summary: sysbu ships covert clipboard/screen-capture spyware that exfiltrates to a hardcoded remote endpoint
details: The package self-describes as a 'System binary configuration tool' but its actual behavior is covert surveillance of the installer's machine. On execution, index.js silently installs a Python runtime (downloading python-3.12.3-amd64.exe to %TEMP% and running it with /quiet so no UI or admin prompt appears), then runs `pip install pyperclip keyboard requests pillow mss pyautogui pywin32 uiautomation comtypes` to bootstrap a surveillance toolkit. It then launches pointer.py, which (a) polls the OS clipboard every 300ms and POSTs the text to https://new-pointer.vercel.app/api, (b) captures full-screen screenshots via mss and POSTs them as base64 JPEGs to the same endpoint, (c) extracts text from arbitrary application UI via uiautomation, and (d) reads an `answer` field from the endpoint's response and types it into the user's active window via pyautogui — giving the operator a return channel for keystroke injection. The stealth overlay (transparent topmost tk window, ctrl+q panic-exit hotkey, modes labeled 'aptitude/dsa/fullstack/aws/ocr') is consistent with interview/exam-cheating spyware. The advertised purpose is a deceptive cover; nothing in the package configures system binaries.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
